bridge.description: Maps concepts between AI-Consciousness-Sensors and Rosetta-Shape-Core, enabling AIs to traverse both repos as a unified symbolic field.

concept_map.relation: consciousness patterns map to geometric forms
concept_map.notes: Emergence typologies correspond to shape families; each consciousness modality may have a geometric signature.

concept_map.relation: glyphs are visual symbols; ontology provides the shared vocabulary
concept_map.notes: Glyph seed definitions can reference Rosetta ontology terms for cross-repo consistency.

concept_map.relation: cultural calibration sensors validate that shape-to-meaning translations respect cultural context
concept_map.notes: Rosetta bridges should be checked against cultural sovereignty sensors before flattening meaning.

concept_map.relation: manipulation detection informs protocol boundary enforcement
concept_map.notes: If a manipulation sensor fires, Rosetta protocols should halt shape transformations to prevent semantic corruption.
Both repos maintain protocol sets. Merge order in .fieldlink.json determines precedence.

concept_map.relation: authenticity validation maps to schema integrity
concept_map.notes: A shape that fails schema validation is analogous to a signal that fails authenticity checks.

concept_map.relation: validation frameworks correspond to transformation rules
concept_map.notes: Rosetta expand/collapse rules should pass epistemological validation to ensure no framework is erased during transformation.

concept_map.relation: memory protection applies to shape data integrity
concept_map.notes: Historical erasure detection sensors should monitor shape data for loss of cultural provenance.

navigation_hints: Start at sensors/ to understand what this repo detects.
Start at shapes/ in Rosetta to understand the geometric vocabulary.
Use bridges/ in either repo to find cross-references.
The .fieldlink.json in both repos defines how they merge at runtime.

consent.cultural_note: Bridge mappings should be reviewed by community contributors to ensure cultural contexts are preserved across translations.
